title: runtime IDE local subagents economy
purpose: I1.2 defines runtime ide local subagents economy for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I1.2` only; siblings under `I1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: When spawn_workers is true and the conductor needs bounded context, orchestrate-subagents spawns economy-tier Task, explore, or shell workers with contracts that cap allowed_reads and forbid journal or state.json writes.
Before each spawn, route-tier.py reads state.json and sets model_tier so workers run at economy tier—not ad-hoc model picks per invocation.
Parallel local subagents start only when the integration manifest lists independent lanes per [C4.2](C4.2-orchestrate-program.md); otherwise the conductor serializes worker turns.
Workers return summaries to the parent; the conductor alone merges results and dual-writes journal and state.json after the pipeline step completes.
Failed or timed-out worker runs append to [H5](H5-worker-runs.md); persistent failures surface as H2 blockers without marking the pursuit step complete.

Edge case `I1.2` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `I1.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.

**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.

narrative: I1.2 defines runtime ide local subagents economy for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system when spawn_workers is true and the conductor needs bounded context, orchestrate-subagents spawns economy-tier task, explore, or shell workers with contracts that cap allowed_reads and forbid journal or state.json writes, then before each spawn, route-tier.py reads state.json and sets model_tier so workers run at economy tier—not ad-hoc model picks per invocation.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

Local subagents run as economy-tier Task/explore/shell workers under conductor contracts with capped allowed_reads. The IDE runtime spawns them via orchestrate-subagents; workers return summaries without touching journal or state.json.

Parallel local subagents are allowed only when integration manifest declares independent lanes ([C4.2](C4.2-orchestrate-program.md)). Model tier comes from route-tier.py output, not ad-hoc per spawn. Failed worker runs log to [H5](H5-worker-runs.md) audit trail.
